raw_text:
**Bugonia, 2025 - ★★★★½**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/6/2/5/4/0/0/625400-bugonia-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=4c6649150e)

Just saying. I’m totally blown away.Bugonia delivers yet another powerhouse performance from Yorgos Lanthimos’s ensemble, and once again, he strikes that perfect alchemy of dark comedy, mundane absurdity, hilariously macabre moments, and razor-sharp unpredictability. It’s the kind of masterful storytelling we saw in Poor Things, and here, he’s arguably at the top of his game.

Emma Stone? Radiant. Just like in Poor Things. But the one I want for president is Jesse Plemons. His performance is unreal, genuinely unsettling in the best way, and absolutely awards-worthy. I haven’t seen him this good since Civil War, and honestly, the man’s been stacking iconic performances since Breaking Bad years ago.

What begins as a darkly comedic slice of modern Americana, a bizarre ménage à trois locked in a twisted little dance macabre, suddenly shifts gears, and the laugh you had gets stuck right in your throat.

By the time the credits roll, you’re left with that rare, electric feeling, like you’ve just witnessed something daring, dangerous, and deeply original. Lanthimos doesn’t just tell stories. He dares you to feel them, even when you don’t want to. Excellence! The Horror!

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/bugonia/

**Mission: Impossible – The Final Reckoning, 2025 - ★★★½**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/5/0/3/4/0/3/503403-mission-impossible-the-final-reckoning-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=00eae3744d)

Watched on Wednesday October 29, 2025.

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/mission-impossible-the-final-reckoning/

**One Battle After Another, 2025 - ★★★★★**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/9/5/1/2/7/7/951277-one-battle-after-another-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=d27c4cc662)

It will not ever be better than this. I must say honestly I had my doubts going into this. Didn't care that much for Leonardo DiCaprios character in the trailer and my overhyped alarm was tingling.

But boy was I wrong! Paul T Anderson is always creating Intense on-screen worlds, with larger than life characters. And for almost three hours his wild ride through contemporary America delivers  a true masterpiece. Very funny, very political and very violent.

Stellar cast with Sean Penn finally returning. He punches u with such a brute force of scary good acting that you'll remember it 4 a very long time. Benicio Del Toro is perfect in his Kung-Fu master of disaster role elegantly and very funny saving the day.

And yes, once again Leonardo lands the biggest role so far of his life, and somehow making it all perfectly believable and very, very funny and at the same time heartbreaking. Biggest kudos goes to Teyana Taylor for a powerful performance as the leader of the resistance and Sean Penn's antagonist. Maybe the brightest shining star of the movie is Chase Infiniti, excellent as DiCaprios daughter, suddenly fighting for her life.

Just go and watch this in the Theater now! 🙌

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/one-battle-after-another/

**After the Hunt, 2025 - ★★**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/1/1/4/9/1/4/0/1149140-after-the-hunt-2025-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=8a38a79f2c)

Unfortunately this is not a good experience at all, way too long and frankly very boring, sadly u don’t feel anything 4 the characters at play. Feels like they’re just going through the motions. The main Ensemble of Julia Roberts, Andrew Garfield,  Ayo Edebiri and Michael Stuhlbarg is doing what they can, and some of the trademarks of director Luca Guadagnino shines through , like the good soundtrack by Trent Reznor and some nice Cinema Verite camera angles and shots. But all in all except Stuhlbargs excellent performance as Julia Roberts husband, this is so bad! Watch it on TV, don’t bother with the cinema treatment 😎

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/after-the-hunt-2025/

**The Hitcher, 1986 - ★★★★★**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/sm/upload/pi/lf/zx/fe/pdPKlwAOX66Zqktp1amFooovAjT-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=bb08ab1974)

One of the true masterpieces of eighties action-thrillers. Written by Eric Red and directed by Robert Harmon. Classic performance by Dutch actor Ruther Hauer as the psycho drifter hitcher who is picked up by a naive young man on the road one dark night, superbly played by C. Thomas Howell. Everyone involved in the Hitcher made their life’s best work. Great score by Mark Isham and the photography is excellent. A must see, still great! Enjoy this together with Near Dark, which is very close in style and also written by Eric Red 🤘

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/the-hitcher/

**Gladiator II, 2024 - ★★½**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/4/8/6/9/7/8/486978-gladiator-ii-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=0fb2edf525)

Ridley recreates his old masterpiece with a simple formula, copyright everything and tell the same story again but 30 years later with the son of Maximus from the first film doing the exact same Journey. A good try with excellent visuals and action and this time around even Denzel Washington cannot save this tired old copycat. Sad 😔

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/gladiator-ii/

**The Brutalist, 2024 - ★★★**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/4/7/8/4/2/8/478428-the-brutalist-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=2f8cae2ea5)

Fascinating, beautiful and gripping drama. Great performances by Felicity Jones, Guy Pearce and Adrien Brody. But way too long 😉

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/the-brutalist/

**Aliens, 1986 - ★★★★★**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/5/1/4/4/5/51445-aliens-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=6c62918bdd)

Special Edition of Aliens by J. Cameron. At last a chance of seeing this true sci-fi action/horror classic in the cinemas again. One of the best in the Alien Saga. Still packs a punch! ✌️👌🤘

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/aliens/

**Sinners, 2025 - ★★★**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/1/1/1/6/6/0/0/1116600-sinners-2025-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=5996b7d555)

Interesting take on the old classic Vampire Horror Theme. Really two films in one, first half paints a vivid colorful canvas depicting the old Black American South of injustice, poverty, religion and music. Latter part is like souped up version of Rodriguez/Tarantinos “From Dusk till Dawn”. Brilliant soundtrack and performances from the ensemble. Great fun!

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/sinners-2025/

**28 Years Later, 2025 - ★★★★**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/9/9/2/7/8/6/992786-28-years-later-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=014e09c066)

Finally the old masters Boyle & Garland, are back with their brilliant minds and visuals. Great reboot and a true comeback to form and a promising continuation of the gruesome Zombie saga that begun with “28 days later” in 2002 🤘✊

Original Link: https://letterboxd.com/tompat1/film/28-years-later/

**Superman, 2025 - ★★★½**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/9/5/7/0/5/0/957050-superman-2025-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=54e41a55ff)

Great comeback! Back to the old, proven strong values and a great storyline. Gunn accomplished the necessary re-boot under impossible circumstances and expectations. Well done!

**Weapons, 2025 - ★★★★**

![Poster](https://a.ltrbxd.com/resized/film-poster/9/7/2/1/0/9/972109-weapons-2025-0-600-0-900-crop.jpg?v=8100270337)

Scary as hell, and funny actually. Love this small town drama with a Stephen King feel. Great performances from the lead actors Julia ”Ozark” Garner and Josh ”Sicario” Brolin. A must see!

Jon Hamm Reacts to His Viral Meme Moment
Jon Hamm has shared his reaction after a dancing scene from Your Friends & Neighbors turned him into a viral meme online.
The clip, showing Hamm closing his eyes and vibing to DJ Kato’s “Turn The Lights Off”, blew up on TikTok in late 2025, inspiring thousands of copycat videos. 
Speaking at a recent premiere, Hamm called the moment “mystifying and adorable,” adding that even though he didn’t get paid for it, he’s happy to be known for something fun and positive instead of negative headlines.
#JonHamm #YourFriendsAndNeighbors #ViralMeme #TikTokViral #MovieNews #TVNews #BehindTheScenes #LetsTalkMovies
With a world-class cast that will have you constantly saying ‘hey, it’s that guy!’, this horror drama about a doomed Royal Navy expedition is a grand treatise on colonial folly

The cast of Mike Flanagan’s ‘THE EXORCIST’:
• Scarlett Johansson
• Jacobi Jupe
• Kate Siegel 
• Rahul Kohli
• Hamish Linklater 
• Samantha Sloyan
• Diane Lane
• Laurence Fishburne
In theatres March 12, 2027.

Brukar du se film på dvd? Intresset för dvd-filmer och Blu-ray växer, och det senaste året har försäljningen tagit fart på flera håll.
Hos auktionssajten Tradera har försäljningen av fysisk film ökat med 25 procent på ett år. Och fem år efter att den sista stora kedjan slutade hyra ut dvd-film i Sverige har en ny hyrvideobutik öppnat i Malmö.
– Jag är så himla trött på streamingtjänster. Det kändes som att de försökte ta min tid, säger Francisco, som just har lämnat tillbaka två dvd:er. 
Hör mer i Kulturnytt: https://www.sverigesradio.se/.../dvd-filmen-pa-frammarsch...

![Archive Image](/assets/journal-images/facebook-assets/386201384739926/video_942631475361778/og_image_thumbnail.jpg)

The scene is from Halt and Catch Fire, AMC’s drama about the 1980s-90s personal computer and internet revolution that aired from 2014 to 2017. AMC used it to promote the premiere of The Audacity, a new darkly comedic tech satire that premiered this month (April 12, 2026) on AMC and AMC+.
The Audacity was created by Jonathan Glatzer (Succession, Better Call Saul). Billy Magnussen plays a flailing data-mining tech CEO navigating power, a mental breakdown, and an unorthodox relationship with his therapist. The cast includes Zach Galifianakis, Sarah Goldberg, Lucy Punch, Simon Helberg, and Rob Corddry.
AMC’s promo framed it as: “Ambition, ego, disruption. Before tonight’s premiere, revisit where the hustle of workplace culture started with Halt and Catch Fire.”
The connection between the 2 shows: both are about tech culture’s capacity to reward the people willing to break the rules. Halt and Catch Fire set that story in the 1980s. The Audacity updates it for Silicon Valley in 2026.
The Audacity screened at SXSW on March 14, 2026 and has already been renewed for Season 2.

![Archive Image](/assets/journal-images/facebook-assets/386201384739926/hash_1525508586/hash_1525508586_image_1.jpg)

29 years later, Event Horizon is finally getting a sequel — and it has the perfect writer
  More in the comments

![Archive Image](/assets/journal-images/facebook-assets/386201384739926/hash_-1188971915/hash_-1188971915_image_1.jpg)

#TheTerror Season 3 is finally coming to AMC+, and we have an exclusive look at the first five minutes of the show. The series was one of the best horror TV shows of the 2010s: Season 1 was a fictionalized account of Captain Sir John Franklin’s real-life expedition to the Arctic in the mid-180s, while Season 2, subtitled Infamy, took place at a U.S. internment camp for Japanese Americans during World War II. Both seasons dealt with scary, supernatural creatures – the first had a terrifying spirit bear known as the Tuunbaq, while the second was the bakemono from Japanese folklore.
The third season, titled Devil in Silver, looks set to continue that in some way. The latest iteration of the anthology show stars Dan Stevens (The Guest, Legion) as Pepper, a working-class man who is wrongfully committed to the New Hyde Psychiatric Hospital. Once there, he uncovers dark secrets, has to combat its patients, and learns of the real terror lurking within its walls. It is based on the novel by Victor LaValle, who serves as a writer and executive producer on the series, alongside Chris Cantwell (who co-created AMC’s Halt and Catch Fire). 
While Stevens’ Pepper isn’t featured in the opening scene, it’s nonetheless a tantalizing hint at what to expect, and suggests the series will be suitably, well, terrifying. It focuses on two staff members at New Hyde having to deal with the dead body of a patient, who has apparently torn himself to death. All we see of that are bloody bedsheets, but the appearance of him underneath is enough to shock the workers. They have to remove him, but his limbs are sticking out and frozen in place, meaning they have to break his bones to fit him through the door, in what’s a gruesome scene that is not for the squeamish.
That’s horrifying enough, but there are some teases that there’s more going on here. The body itself suggests something supernatural could be at play, and it’s even posited that he might not have done it to himself, which begs the question of who, or what, did. Ominously, the camera also lingers on the patient’s room number: 217. That’s the same as the most haunted room at the Overlook Hotel from Stephen King’s The Shining book, which is an Easter egg designed to tell us that some kind of evil spirit lingers there. Given the title, could it even be the devil itself?
It’s all suitably spooky, and very much looks like it’s living up to its predecessors. There’s also no need to fear if you haven’t seen the previous seasons, as the anthology format is designed for this to be a standalone story. The Terror: Devil in Silver premieres on May 7th on AMC+ and Shudder.
Check out the exclusive clip in the comments...

Michael Mann’s genre-defining thriller celebrates its 40th anniversary!
Newly titled MANHUNTER: THE FINAL CUT, the film returns in a stunning 4K restoration, supervised by Michael Mann and debuting on June 12 at The Academy Museum of Motion Pictures. The international premiere follows on June 24 at Cineteca di Bologna, ahead of the US release by Rialto Pictures, in addition to select territories from September.
